questions.question: What is meant by the particulate nature of matter?
questions.answer: It means that matter is made up of extremely small particles which cannot be seen with the naked eye.

questions.question: How does grinding chalk demonstrate the particulate nature of matter?
questions.answer: Grinding chalk into smaller pieces shows that matter can be divided into smaller particles without changing its nature.

questions.question: What conclusion can be drawn from the sugar dissolving experiment?
questions.answer: It shows that particles of matter mix with each other and have spaces between them.

questions.question: What are interparticle spaces?
questions.answer: They are the empty spaces present between particles of matter.

questions.question: What are interparticle forces of attraction?
questions.answer: They are forces that hold particles of matter together.

questions.question: Why do solids have a definite shape and volume?
questions.answer: Because particles in solids are closely packed and strongly attracted to each other.

questions.question: Why do liquids flow easily?
questions.answer: Liquids flow easily because their particles can move around each other.

questions.question: Why do gases expand to fill the entire container?
questions.answer: Gas particles move freely in all directions and spread throughout the available space.

questions.question: Why are gases easily compressible?
questions.answer: Because there are large spaces between gas particles.

questions.question: What does the syringe experiment demonstrate?
questions.answer: It demonstrates that gases are compressible because their particles can be pushed closer together.

questions.question: What is diffusion?
questions.answer: Diffusion is the movement of particles from a region of higher concentration to lower concentration.

questions.question: Why does diffusion occur faster in gases?
questions.answer: Because gas particles move faster and are farther apart than particles in liquids or solids.

questions.question: How does temperature affect diffusion?
questions.answer: Higher temperature increases particle motion and speeds up diffusion.

questions.question: What is melting?
questions.answer: Melting is the process in which a solid changes into a liquid on heating.

questions.question: What happens to particle movement during melting?
questions.answer: Particles gain energy and begin to move more freely.

questions.question: What is boiling point?
questions.answer: It is the temperature at which a liquid changes into gas throughout the liquid.

questions.question: What is evaporation?
questions.answer: Evaporation is the slow conversion of a liquid into vapor from its surface.

questions.question: Name two factors that increase evaporation.
questions.answer: High temperature and moving air increase evaporation.

questions.question: What are fluids?
questions.answer: Fluids are substances that can flow easily, such as liquids and gases.

questions.question: Why does perfume smell spread quickly in a room?
questions.answer: Because perfume particles diffuse rapidly through air.

questions.question: What happens to interparticle forces when matter changes from solid to liquid?
questions.answer: The interparticle forces become weaker.

questions.question: Why do gases have low density?
questions.answer: Because their particles are far apart with large spaces between them.

questions.question: Which state of matter has the fastest particle motion?
questions.answer: Gases have the fastest particle motion.

questions.question: What happens to particle spacing when matter changes from solid to gas?
questions.answer: The spacing between particles increases greatly.

questions.question: State one important conclusion of particle theory.
questions.answer: Matter consists of particles that are in constant motion and have spaces between them.
